title: Listening - Part 3: Preparing for an Important Occasion
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about preparing for an important occasion. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H what each speaker found most helpful while preparing. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about preparing for an important occasion. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H what each speaker found most helpful while preparing. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people talk about preparing for an important occasion.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: I had to give a speech at my sister's wedding. I wrote one full of funny memories and assumed it would be perfect, but before the day I read it to a close friend. [19]She gently pointed out that two stories would only make sense to our family and suggested where I should shorten the speech, so I knew the guests would actually enjoy listening.[/19] I was nervous at the wedding, but people laughed at the right moments and my sister said it was exactly the right length.||Speaker 2 Man: My final music exam involved performing on a piano at an arts centre, not on the instrument I normally use at home. I was worried that the unfamiliar room and piano would affect me. [20]Being allowed to rehearse there the previous afternoon was invaluable, because I got used to the sound, the seat position and the walk onto the stage before the examination itself.[/20] I still made a small mistake, but I didn't feel distracted or uncomfortable, and my overall mark was better than I expected.||Speaker 3 Woman: I organised my father's surprise birthday party and initially tried to handle invitations, food, decorations and music by myself. After a week, I was exhausted and had forgotten several details. [21]The turning point was giving specific jobs to my brother and cousins, so one arranged food, another collected photographs and somebody else prepared the music.[/21] Once I stopped controlling everything, the planning became manageable and the party was better than anything I could have organised alone. My father didn't suspect a thing.||Speaker 4 Man: I entered a formal job interview for the first time and could find endless advice online, much of it contradictory. My aunt has interviewed applicants for years, so I asked whether she would talk through the process with me. [22]Her experience meant she could explain the kinds of examples employers look for and practise likely questions with me in a realistic way.[/22] I didn't memorise answers, but I knew how to describe my skills clearly, and I was offered the job two days later.||Speaker 5 Woman: For a charity cake sale, I planned to make an enormous decorated cake shaped like a castle. The design looked impressive online, but my first attempt collapsed in the kitchen and took hours. [23]I eventually decided to bake smaller individual cakes with simple decoration instead, which meant I could produce enough on time and avoid worrying that everything would fall apart during transport.[/23] They sold quickly, and I raised more money than expected. I learned that an ambitious idea isn't always the most useful one.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: I had to give a speech at my sister's wedding. I wrote one full of funny memories and assumed it would be perfect, but before the day I read it to a close friend. She gently pointed out that two stories would only make sense to our family and suggested where I should shorten the speech, so I knew the guests would actually enjoy listening. I was nervous at the wedding, but people laughed at the right moments and my sister said it was exactly the right length.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: My final music exam involved performing on a piano at an arts centre, not on the instrument I normally use at home. I was worried that the unfamiliar room and piano would affect me. Being allowed to rehearse there the previous afternoon was invaluable, because I got used to the sound, the seat position and the walk onto the stage before the examination itself. I still made a small mistake, but I didn't feel distracted or uncomfortable, and my overall mark was better than I expected.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: I organised my father's surprise birthday party and initially tried to handle invitations, food, decorations and music by myself. After a week, I was exhausted and had forgotten several details. The turning point was giving specific jobs to my brother and cousins, so one arranged food, another collected photographs and somebody else prepared the music. Once I stopped controlling everything, the planning became manageable and the party was better than anything I could have organised alone. My father didn't suspect a thing.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: I entered a formal job interview for the first time and could find endless advice online, much of it contradictory. My aunt has interviewed applicants for years, so I asked whether she would talk through the process with me. Her experience meant she could explain the kinds of examples employers look for and practise likely questions with me in a realistic way. I didn't memorise answers, but I knew how to describe my skills clearly, and I was offered the job two days later.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: For a charity cake sale, I planned to make an enormous decorated cake shaped like a castle. The design looked impressive online, but my first attempt collapsed in the kitchen and took hours. I eventually decided to bake smaller individual cakes with simple decoration instead, which meant I could produce enough on time and avoid worrying that everything would fall apart during transport. They sold quickly, and I raised more money than expected. I learned that an ambitious idea isn't always the most useful one.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: A friend's truthful comments helped the speaker improve a wedding speech for the audience.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: A rehearsal in the exam venue helped the speaker feel familiar with the performance conditions.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Dividing practical preparations among family members made organising the party manageable.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: The aunt's interviewing experience gave the speaker useful preparation for a first formal interview.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: A simpler baking plan made it possible for the speaker to prepare enough cakes reliably and on time.
